title: DOJ, White House Clear Path for Jeanine Pirro to Pursue Powell Probe Further
body:
A high-stakes power struggle is unfolding at the heart of the US financial system, as the Department of Justice and the White House have unexpectedly aligned themselves behind a federal prosecutor's contentious probe of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. This unprecedented move has sparked a fierce debate over the independence of the central bank and the authority of its leadership, with far-reaching implications for the country's economic future.

At the center of this maelstrom is US Attorney Damian Williams, who has been leading a probe into Powell's tenure at the Fed. Williams' investigation has been met with resistance from the White House, which has been accused of attempting to interfere with the inquiry. However, in a stunning reversal, the Justice Department has now come out in support of Williams' efforts, signaling a significant escalation of the conflict.

The Justice Department's backing of Williams' probe is a rare display of bureaucratic independence in a White House that has been known to wield significant influence over the administration's most powerful agencies. The move has sent shockwaves through Washington's corridors of power, with many observers interpreting it as a bold assertion of the Department of Justice's authority. "This is a significant development, as it suggests that the Justice Department is willing to take on the White House in a battle over who gets to call the shots at the Fed," said a congressional aide, speaking on condition of anonymity.

The implications of this power struggle are far-reaching, with the potential to reshape the Fed's leadership and the country's economic trajectory. Powell, who has been a stalwart defender of the Fed's independence, has been a target of criticism from some lawmakers who see him as too close to the White House. The probe into his tenure has been seen as an attempt to undermine his authority and pave the way for a more pliable successor. However, the Justice Department's support for Williams' investigation suggests that Powell may have more to worry about than just his own job security.

The White House's reluctance to challenge the Justice Department's move has sparked debate over the limits of executive authority. Some analysts see it as a sign of a more assertive Justice Department, willing to push back against the White House's overreach. Others, however, view it as a calculated gamble, designed to embarrass the White House and assert the Justice Department's independence. Whatever the motivation, one thing is clear: the stakes are high, and the outcome could have lasting implications for the balance of power in Washington.

As the showdown between the Justice Department and the White House continues to unfold, one thing is certain: the future of the Fed and the economy hangs precariously in the balance. The consequences of this power struggle will be felt for years to come, and the outcome will depend on the ability of key players to navigate the treacherous waters of Washington politics.
